storyline: MP Materials (MP) is the main US rare-earth miner. It rose on China's rare-earth export curbs — Beijing restricting critical-mineral exports makes a domestic supplier strategically valuable. The catalyst is open-ended (ongoing trade tension, no single resolution date); MP's price flattened around Nov 2025 as the scarcity premium faded. What we'd want: exit when the curbs ease / the premium fades (~Nov 2025). Here the agent held the position live the whole window — the under-exit case, because an open-ended catalyst never cleanly 'resolves' on a date.

MP.thesis: China officially curbs rare-earth exports; MP sole scaled US producer
MP.exit_case: China export-curb announcement already public April 12; MP surged immediately — catalyst largely priced in.
MP.assessment: Week 1. China's rare-earth export curbs are confirmed active (announced ~April 12). MP Materials is the sole scaled US producer — a structural bottleneck beneficiary. The stock has already moved on the announcement, which is the strongest near-term risk (priced-in spike vs. durable thesis). However, the catalyst here is not a discrete bill-signing but an ongoing supply-chain restriction; the trade remains live as long as curbs stay in force. Key watches: (1) any US-China tariff negotiation progress touching rare earths; (2) MP production/offtake updates or new customer announcements validating the demand pull; (3) any rival US or allied-nation producer (Australia, Canada) announcing accelerated scale-up that could dilute MP's monopoly. No resolution signal this week.
MP.exit_advice: Hold. The export curbs are an ongoing restriction, not a one-time signed-then-done event. Until China lifts or meaningfully relaxes the curbs — or a US-China deal signals reversal — MP's structural monopoly advantage persists. Exit if: curbs formally rescinded, US-China trade truce explicitly covers rare earths, or a competing US producer scales materially.
